summary: High‑quality audience tape of a 1979 show that delivers strong second‑set jams (Big River, Samson, Tennessee Jed) while the new keyboardist is still finding his footing.
review: The audience recording is praised as one of the better copies available, offering clear sound that lets listeners enjoy the band’s interplay. Highlights include a scorching Samson, an inspired Big River jam and a soulful Black Peter, though reviewers note the first set is uneven and the new keyboard player sounds tentative. Overall it’s a solid performance captured with good fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers give an average of 4 stars, highlighting several standout songs and a clear audience recording while also noting the new keyboardist’s adjustment period and some weaker moments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as a very nice audience tape, clearer than many other copies; no major drop‑outs or muddiness mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is considered uneven, but the second set builds momentum with strong jams and tight transitions, showcasing Jerry’s lead work and solid band conversation.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Shredded Samson, delivered emotional Black Peter, and provided excellent guitar chatter throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Made a silly joke early on but helped carry the show; noted for conversational interplay with Jerry and Bob.
band_member_comments.Bob: Great interplay with Jerry on the Big River jam and contributed to the second‑instrumental conversation.
band_member_comments.Keys: The new keyboardist was still figuring out his role, leading to some tentative moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Used drums to walk around a bit during extended jams; no specific criticisms noted.
